key_insight: The paper adds computable two-sided a posteriori error certificates for PINNs solving ODEs by pairing new lower bounds with improved localized upper bounds, without needing the exact solution.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable PINN deployment in safety- or verification-sensitive simulation settings by quantifying when predictions are provably close to the true ODE solution.
method: Derive lower error bounds from localized strong monotonicity on certified state-space domains.
Derive sharper upper bounds using a one-sided Lipschitz condition and local growth constants.
For linear systems, express certificates via eigenvalues of the symmetric part and use signed-residual finite-probe tests when initial conditions are enforced exactly.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports analytical derivations and explicit formulas for ODEs and linear time-invariant/time-varying systems, rather than numerical benchmark results.
watch_item: Certificates depend on verifiable local monotonicity/domain assumptions, and exact hard enforcement of initial conditions can make the scalar lower bound uninformative.
paper_title: Reliable Error Estimation for PINNs: Lower and Upper A Posteriori Bounds
